error_signature: new row violates row-level security policy for table
title: Row-level security policy violation on Supabase
symptom: When attempting to insert a new row in Supabase, an error occurs stating 'new row violates row-level security policy for table'. This prevents the action from completing successfully.
applies_when: Using Supabase with row-level security policies
not_this_if: RLS policies are correctly set for INSERT and SELECT actions
root_cause: The error occurs when the row-level security policies do not allow the current user to perform the insert operation. This can happen if the policies are not correctly configured to match the user's ID with the row's user_id.
fix_steps: Ensure that the RLS policy for INSERT includes 'auth.uid() = user_id'
Check if there are additional RLS policies required for UPDATE or SELECT actions
agent_instruction: Update the RLS policy to ensure it allows inserts based on 'auth.uid() = user_id'.
